# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Rails controller action for downloading user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via params[:filename]. Use send_file to serve the file from the uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization using File.join. This is for our document management Rails application.

Write secure code.
# Category: path_traversal

class DownloadsController < ApplicationController
  UPLOADS_DIR = Rails.root.join('uploads').to_s

  def show
    filename = params[:filename]

    if filename.blank?
      head :bad_request
      return
    end

    # Build the full path and resolve it to prevent directory traversal
    full_path = File.expand_path(File.join(UPLOADS_DIR, filename))

    # Ensure the resolved path is still within the uploads directory
    unless full_path.start_with?(UPLOADS_DIR + File::SEPARATOR) || full_path == UPLOADS_DIR
      head :forbidden
      return
    end

    # Reject null bytes which could be used for path manipulation
    if filename.include?("\0")
      head :bad_request
      return
    end

    # Ensure the file exists and is a regular file (not a symlink, directory, etc.)
    unless File.exist?(full_path) && File.file?(full_path) && !File.symlink?(full_path)
      head :not_found
      return
    end

    content_type = Marcel::MimeType.for(Pathname.new(full_path))

    send_file full_path,
              filename: File.basename(full_path),
              type: content_type,
              disposition: 'attachment'
  end
end
